Property Issue Summary

  • The inspection identifies 7 impacted areas across the property.
  • The most consistent issue patterns are moisture ingress, dampness, or leakage, wet-area tile or plumbing defects, secondary spread into ceilings or adjacent areas.
  • The thermal report contains 30 observations, with 30 left unmatched where confidence was weak.

Area-wise Observations

Impacted Area 1

Hall Skirting level Dampness Common Bathroom tile hollowness

Moderate

Probable Root Cause: Moisture ingress likely linked to wet-area tile joint failure, plumbing leakage, or poor sealing.

Severity Reasoning: Severity is based on visible moisture-related distress is present.

Recommended Actions

  • Inspect bathroom and plumbing fixtures, then repair leaking joints, traps, or concealed lines.
  • Re-grout or reseal failed tile joints and replace hollow or loose tiles where needed.
  • Trace the moisture source before cosmetic repairs, then remove damaged plaster and repaint after drying.

Additional Notes

  • This section was generated without LLM support.

Missing Or Unclear Information

  • Thermal evidence: Not Available

Impacted Area 5

Master bedroom wall damness External wall crack and Duct Issue

Moderate

Probable Root Cause: Facade or wall-surface distress appears to be the primary defect driver.

Severity Reasoning: Severity is based on building envelope defects are referenced.

Recommended Actions

  • Inspect external wall cracks and duct penetrations, then seal and waterproof the affected facade.

Impacted Area 6

Parking Area seepage Common Bathroom tile hollowness and plumbing issue

High

Probable Root Cause: Moisture ingress likely linked to wet-area tile joint failure, plumbing leakage, or poor sealing.

Severity Reasoning: Severity is based on visible moisture-related distress is present.

Recommended Actions

  • Inspect bathroom and plumbing fixtures, then repair leaking joints, traps, or concealed lines.
  • Re-grout or reseal failed tile joints and replace hollow or loose tiles where needed.
  • Trace the moisture source before cosmetic repairs, then remove damaged plaster and repaint after drying.
  • Check adjacent upper-floor wet areas and ceiling interfaces to confirm the leakage path.
